Identification.LongTitle: An Act to amend the Department of Foreign Affairs, Trade and Development Act, the Justice for Victims of Corrupt Foreign Officials Act (Sergei Magnitsky Law) and the Special Economic Measures Act
Identification.ShortTitle: Sergei Magnitsky International Anti-Corruption and Human Rights Act
Identification.RunningHead: International Anti-Corruption and Human Rights Act (Sergei Magnitsky Law No. 3)

Stages.StageText: Reprinted as amended by the Standing Committee on Foreign Affairs and International Development as a working copy for the use of the House of Commons at Report Stage and as reported to the House on June 18, 2026

Provision.Text: This enactment amends the Department of Foreign Affairs, Trade and Development Act to impose certain requirements on the Minister of Foreign Affairs in relation to international human rights, prisoners of conscience and human rights defenders.
It also amends the Justice for Victims of Corrupt Foreign Officials Act (Sergei Magnitsky Law) to provide that

Provision.Label: (c)
Provision.Text: a copy of each order or regulation made under paragraph 4(1)(b) of the Act must be tabled in each House of Parliament; and

Provision.Label: (f)
Provision.Text: the punishment under paragraph 11(b) of the Act be increased.

It also amends the Special Economic Measures Act to change that Actâs short title to the âGlobal Sanctions and Special Economic Measures Act (Sergei Magnitski Law No. 2)â. It further amends that Act to provide that

Provision.Label: (d)
Provision.Text: every order or regulation made under paragraph 4(1)(b) of the Act must be tabled in each House of Parliament; and

Provision.Label: (e)
Provision.Text: the punishment under paragraph 8(a) of the Act be increased.

Whereas Parliament supports strengthening Canadaâs sanctions regime to better align with ally countries through the imposition of economic measures against a person, entity, foreign national or foreign state that has engaged in activities that undermine international peace, security or stability in a manner inconsistent with international law or who is responsible for, or complicit in, gross and systemic violations of internationally recognized human rights or acts of corruption;
Whereas Parliament considers that it is desirable that the Government of Canada continue working with ally countries towards establishing the International Anti-Corruption Court;

And whereas this Act will enable Canada to impose sanctions on foreign nationals who have engaged in activities that undermine international peace, security or stability in a manner inconsistent with international law or who are responsible for, or complicit in, gross and systematic violations of internationally recognized human rights or acts of corruption;
Now, therefore, His Majesty, by and with the advice and consent of the Senate and House of Commons of Canada, enacts as follows:

Section.Text: This Act may be cited as the International Anti-Corruption and Human Rights Act (Sergei Magnitsky Law No. 3).

Heading.TitleText: Department of Foreign Affairs, Trade and Development Act

Section.Text: Section 10 of the Department of Foreign Affairs, Trade and Development Act is amended by adding the following after subsection (3):

Subsection.Text: In exercising the powers and performing the duties and functions set out in this Act in respect of the conduct of the external affairs of Canada, the Minister must publish, at least once every calendar year, a report that includes an outline of the measures that the Government of Canada has taken to advance human rights â and support prisoners of conscience and human rights defenders â internationally as part of Canadaâs foreign policy.
The Minister must publish the first report under subsection (4) within 12 months after the day on which this subsection comes into force.

In this section, prisoner of conscience means an individual who, in contravention of international human rights standards, has been detained or otherwise physically restricted solely because of their identity or their conscientiously held beliefs, including religious or political beliefs.

Heading.TitleText: Justice for Victims of Corrupt Foreign Officials Act (Sergei Magnitsky Law)

Section.Text: Section 2 of the Justice for Victims of Corrupt Foreign Officials Act (Sergei Magnitsky Law) is amended by adding the following in alphabetical order:

Definition.Text: transnational repressionâmeans tactics used by a foreign state to intimidate, harass, surveil or threaten individuals or groups located outside the state borders or physically harm such individuals or members of such groups, including elected officials, political dissidents, human rights defenders, exiled journalists, diaspora communities, civil society activists and refugees, for the purpose of silencing dissent and stifling activism.â(rÃ©pression transnationale)

Text.Ins: Paragraph 4(2)(a) of the Act is replaced by the following:

Paragraph.Label: (a)
Paragraph.Text: a foreign national is responsible for, or complicit in, gross violations of internationally recognized human rights;

Subsection.Text: Subsection 4(2) of the Act is amended by striking out âorâ at the end of paragraph (c), by adding âorâ at the end of paragraph (e) and by adding the following after paragraph (e):

Paragraph.Label: (e)
Paragraph.Text: a foreign national has committed transnational repression as an agent of or on behalf of a foreign state; or

Paragraph.Label: (f)
Paragraph.Text: a foreign national has engaged in activities that undermine or are likely to undermine international peace, security or stability.

Section.Text: [Deleted]
Section 4.4 of the Act is replaced by the following:

Subsection.Text: Within 24 months of the seizure or restraint of private property under an order made under paragraph 4(1)(b), the Minister must apply for forfeiture of the private property under section 4.2.
The Minister must dispose of the private property forfeited within 30 days of the forfeiture.
Subsections (1) and (2) do not apply in cases in respect of which legal proceedings are ongoing.
Section 5 of the Act is replaced by the following:

A copy of each order or regulation made under paragraph 4(1)(a) or (b) must be tabled in each House of Parliament within 15 days after it is made. It may be sent to the Clerk of the House if the House is not sitting.
[Deleted]
[Deleted]

Section.Text: [Deleted]
[Deleted]
Paragraph 11b) of the Act is replaced by the following:

Paragraph.Label: (b)
Paragraph.Text: is guilty of an offence punishable on summary conviction and is liable to a fine of not more than $100,000 or to imprisonment for a term of not more than three years, or to both.

Section.Text: [Deleted]
Section 1 of the Act is replaced by the following:
This Act may be cited as the Global Sanctions and Special Economic Measures Act (Sergei Magnitsky Law No. 2).
Section 2 of the Act is amended by adding the following in alphabetical order:

Definition.Text: transnational repressionâmeans tactics used by a foreign state to intimidate, harass, surveil or threaten individuals or groups located outside the state borders or to physically harm such individuals or members of such groups, including elected officials, political dissidents, human rights defenders, exiled journalists, diaspora communities, civil society activists and refugees, for the purpose of silencing dissent and stifling activism.â(rÃ©pression transnationale)

Section.Text: Section 3.1 of the Act is replaced by the following:

The purpose of this Act is to enable the Government of Canada to take economic measures against certain persons in circumstances where an international organization of states or association of states of which Canada is a member calls on its members to do so, international peace, security or stability has been undermined or is likely to be undermined, gross and systematic human rights violations have been committed in a foreign state or acts of significant corruption involving a national of a foreign state have been committed.

Subsection.Text: Paragraph 4(1)(a) of the Act is replaced by the following:

Paragraph.Label: (a)
Paragraph.Text: make any orders or regulations with respect to the restriction or prohibition of any of the activities referred to in subsection (2) that the Governor in Council considers necessary; and

Subsection.Text: Paragraph 4(1.1)(b) of the Act is replaced by the following:

Paragraph.Label: (b)
Paragraph.Text: international peace, security or stability has been undermined or is likely to be undermined;

Subsection.Text: Paragraph 4(1.1)(c) of the Act is replaced by the following:

Paragraph.Label: (c)
Paragraph.Text: transnational repression or gross and systematic human rights violations have been committed in a foreign state; or

Subsection.Text: The portion of subsection 4(2) of the English version of the Act before paragraph (a) is replaced by the following:
Orders and regulations may be made under paragraph (1)(a) with respect to the restriction or prohibition of any of the following activities, whether carried out in or outside Canada:

Section.Text: [Deleted]
Section 5.6 of the Act is replaced by the following:

Subsection.Text: Within 24 months of the seizure or restraint of private property under an order made under paragraph 4(1)(b), the Minister must apply for forfeiture of the private property under section 5.4.
The Minister must dispose of the private property forfeited within 30 days of the forfeiture.
Subsections (1) and (2) do not apply in cases in respect of which legal proceedings are ongoing.

Section.Text: [Deleted]
[Deleted]
Subsection 7(1) of the Act is replaced by the following:

Subsection.Text: Every order and regulation made under paragraph 4(1)(a) or (b) shall be laid before each House of Parliament by a member of the Kingâs Privy Council for Canada within five sitting days of that House after it is made.
[Deleted]

Section.Text: Paragraph 8(a) of the Act is replaced by the following:

Paragraph.Label: (a)
Paragraph.Text: is guilty of an offence punishable on summary conviction and is liable to a fine not exceeding one hundred thousand dollars or to imprisonment for a term not exceeding three years, or to both; or

Section.Text: The portion of subsection 10(1) of the Act before paragraph (a) is replaced by the following:

Subsection.Text: The original or a copy of a bill of lading, customs document, commercial invoice or other document is admissible in evidence in a prosecution under this Part in relation to any dealing with respect to goods, or services rendered in relation to these goods, where it appears from the document that

Section.MarginalNote: Replacement of âSpecial Economic Measures Actâ in other Acts

Subsection.Text: Every reference to the âSpecial Economic Measures Actâ is replaced by a reference to the âGlobal Sanctions and Special Economic Measures Act (Sergei Magnitsky Law No. 2)â in the following provisions:

Paragraph.Label: (a)
Paragraph.Text: paragraph 13(3)â(d) of the Seized Property Management Act;

Paragraph.Label: (b)
Paragraph.Text: in the Proceeds of Crime (Money Laundering) and Terrorist Financing Act:

Subparagraph.Label: (i)
Subparagraph.Text: the definition sanctions evasion offence in subsection 2(1),

Paragraph.Label: (c)
Paragraph.Text: paragraphs 35.1(1)(a) and (b) of the Immigration and Refugee Protection Act;

Paragraph.Label: (d)
Paragraph.Text: paragraph 6(2)â(d) of the International Interests in Mobile Equipment (aircraft equipment) Act;

Paragraph.Label: (e)
Paragraph.Text: subsection 20(1) of the Freezing Assets of Corrupt Foreign Officials Act;

Paragraph.Label: (f)
Paragraph.Text: in the Justice for Victims of Corrupt Foreign Officials Act (Sergei Magnitsky Law):

Paragraph.Label: (g)
Paragraph.Text: paragraphs 27(3.1)(a) and (b) of the Online News Act.

Subsection.Text: Unless the context requires otherwise, every reference to the âSpecial Economic Measures Actâ in any provision of an Act of Parliament, other than a provision referred to in subsection (1), is to be read as a reference to the âGlobal Sanctions and Special Economic Measures Act (Sergei Magnitsky Law No. 2)â.

Every reference to the âSpecial Economic Measures Actâ is replaced by a reference to the âGlobal Sanctions and Special Economic Measures Act (Sergei Magnitsky Law No. 2)â in the following provisions:

Paragraph.Label: (a)
Paragraph.Text: paragraph (c) of the definition listed person or entity in subsection 1(2) of the Proceeds of Crime (Money Laundering) and Terrorist Financing Suspicious Transaction Reporting Regulations;

Paragraph.Label: (b)
Paragraph.Text: in the Special Economic Measures (Myanmar) Regulations:

Paragraph.Label: (c)
Paragraph.Text: subsection 10(2) of the Special Economic Measures (Zimbabwe) Regulations;

Paragraph.Label: (d)
Paragraph.Text: subsection 10(2) of the Special Economic Measures (Iran) Regulations;

Paragraph.Label: (e)
Paragraph.Text: in the Regulations Implementing the United Nations Resolutions and Imposing Special Economic Measures on Libya:

Paragraph.Label: (f)
Paragraph.Text: subsection 6(2) of the Special Economic Measures (Syria) Regulations;

Paragraph.Label: (g)
Paragraph.Text: paragraph 4(1)â(c) of the General Export Permit No. 45 â Cryptography for the Development or Production of a Product;

Paragraph.Label: (h)
Paragraph.Text: paragraph 3(1)â(c) of the General Export Permit No. 46 â Cryptography for Use by Certain Consignees;

Paragraph.Label: (i)
Paragraph.Text: subsection 7(2) of the Special Economic Measures (Russia) Regulations;

Paragraph.Label: (j)
Paragraph.Text: subsection 7(2) of the Special Economic Measures (Ukraine) Regulations;

Paragraph.Label: (k)
Paragraph.Text: subsection 7(2) of the Special Economic Measures (South Sudan) Regulations;

Paragraph.Label: (l)
Paragraph.Text: paragraph 3(1)â(c) of the General Export Permit No. 41 â Dual-use Goods and Technology to Certain Destinations;

Paragraph.Label: (m)
Paragraph.Text: subsection 7(2) of the Special Economic Measures (Venezuela) Regulations;

Paragraph.Label: (n)
Paragraph.Text: subsection 7(2) of the Special Economic Measures (Nicaragua) Regulations;

Paragraph.Label: (o)
Paragraph.Text: subsection 7(2) of the Special Economic Measures (Belarus) Regulations;

Paragraph.Label: (p)
Paragraph.Text: subsection 7(2) of the Special Economic Measures (Peopleâs Republic of China) Regulations;

Paragraph.Label: (q)
Paragraph.Text: subsection 7(2) of the Special Economic Measures (Haiti) Regulations;

Paragraph.Label: (r)
Paragraph.Text: subsection 7(2) of the Special Economic Measures (Sri Lanka) Regulations;

Paragraph.Label: (s)
Paragraph.Text: subsection 7(2) of the Special Economic Measures (Moldova) Regulations;

Paragraph.Label: (t)
Paragraph.Text: in the Special Economic Measures (Guatemala) Regulations:

Paragraph.Label: (u)
Paragraph.Text: in the Special Economic Measures (Hamas Terrorist Attacks) Regulations:

Paragraph.Label: (v)
Paragraph.Text: in the Special Economic Measures (Sudan) Regulations:

Paragraph.Label: (w)
Paragraph.Text: in the Special Economic Measures (Extremist Settler Violence) Regulations:

Subsection.Text: Unless the context requires otherwise, every reference to the âSpecial Economic Measures Actâ in any provision of a regulation, as defined in section 2 of the Statutory Instruments Act, made under an Act of Parliament, other than a provision referred to in subsection (1), is to be read as a reference to the âGlobal Sanctions and Special Economic Measures Act (Sergei Magnitsky Law No. 2)â.

Section.Text: [Deleted]
[Deleted]
